name: AutoExpand

unions.name: Config
unions.comment:
Configuration for your textarea, describing the look and feel.

**Note:** Your `Config` should _never_ be held in your model.
It should only appear in `view` code.

unions.name: State
unions.comment: Keeps track of how many rows we need.

values.name: attributes
values.comment:
Get the attributes needed for a custom textarea. Note that you may
accidentally break functionality by including some attributes twice.

    textarea
        ([ placeholder "Custom..." ]
            ++ AutoExpand.attributes
                myConfig
                model.autoExpandState
                model.textValue
        )
        []
values.type: Config msg -> State -> String -> List (Attribute msg)

values.name: config
values.comment:
Create the `Config` for the auto expanding textarea.

A typical configuration might look like this:

    type Msg
        = AutoExpandInput { textValue : String, state : AutoExpand.State }

    config : AutoExpand.Config Msg
    config =
        AutoExpand.config
            { onInput = AutoExpandInput
            , padding = 10
            , lineHeight = 20
            , minRows = 1
            , maxRows = 4
            }
values.type: { onInput : { textValue : String, state : State } -> msg, padding : Float, lineHeight : Float, minRows : Int, maxRows : Int } -> Config msg

values.name: initState
values.comment: Sets up the initial `State` for the textarea using a `Config`.

values.name: view
values.comment:
Show the textarea on your page.

    view : Model -> Html Msg
    view model =
        AutoExpand.view config model.autoExpandState model.textValue
values.type: Config msg -> State -> String -> Html msg

values.name: withClass
values.comment:
Add the `class` attribute to the configuration.

    myConfig
        |> withClass "textarea has-inset-shadow"

values.name: withId
values.comment:
Add the `id` attribute to the configuration.

    myConfig
        |> withId "chat-message-textarea"

values.name: withPlaceholder
values.comment:
Add the `placeholder` attribute to the configuration.

    myConfig
        |> withPlaceholder "Type a message here"

values.name: withStyles
values.comment:
Add inline styles for the textarea.

    myConfig
        |> withStyles [ ( "font-family", "sans-serif" ) ]
values.type: List ( String, String ) -> Config msg -> Config msg
